intended_use: The ECHELON Synergy System is an imaging device and is intended to provide the physician with physiological and clinical information, obtained non-invasively and without the use of ionizing radiation.
intended_use_full: The ECHELON Synergy System is an imaging device and is intended to provide the physician with physiological and clinical information, obtained non-invasively and without the use of ionizing radiation. The MR system produces transverse, coronal, sagittal, oblique, and curved cross sectional images that display the internal structure of the head, body, or extremities.
ground_truth: Three US board-certified radiologists performed blinded review of images reconstructed with conventional method versus DLR Clear, evaluating image quality metrics
other_metrics: DLR Clear: Truncation artifact reduction, Relative Edge Sharpness, and FWHM (Full Width at Half Minimum) demonstrated improvement on phantom testing; clinical reviewer assessment used 3-point scale for truncation artifact reduction, image sharpness, lesion conspicuity, and overall image quality with p<0.05 statistical significance; AutoPose: reduced time and number of steps compared to manual positioning in almost all cases (177 spine cases, 66 breast cases, 65 hip joint cases)
